const FeatureBadge = ({

}: {
  name: string
}) => {
  const getIcon = (featureName: string) => {
    if (featureName.includes("Slack")) {
      return (
        <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="w-4 h-4 opacity-50">
          <path
            d="M6 2C6 1.44772 5.55228 1 5 1C4.44772 1 4 1.44772 4 2V6C4 6.55228 4.44772 7 5 7C5.55228 7 6 6.55228 6 6V2Z"
            fill="#E01E5A"
          />
          <path d="M10 6C10.5523 6 11 5.55228 11 5C11 4.44772 10.55228 4 10 4H6V6H10Z" fill="#36C5F0" />
          <path
            d="M14 5C14 4.44772 13.5523 4 13 4C12.4477 4 12 4.44772 12 5V9C12 9.55228 12.4477 10 13 10C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V5Z"

          />

        </svg>
      )
    } else if (featureName.includes("Meeting")) {
      return (
        <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="w-4 h-4 opacity-50">
          <path
            d="M2 4C2 3.44772 2.44772 3 3 3H9C9.55228 3 10 3.44772 10 4V10C10 10.55228 9.55228 11 9 11H3C2.44772 11 2 10.55228 2 10V4Z"
            stroke="#5E6AD2"
            strokeWidth="1.5"
            strokeLinecap="round"
            strokeLinejoin="round"
          />
          <path
            d="M10 5L13 3V11L10 9"
            stroke="#5E6AD2"
            strokeWidth="1.5"
            strokeLinecap="round"
            strokeLinejoin="round"

        </svg>
      )
    } else if (featureName.includes("Sentiment")) {
      return (
        <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="w-4 h-4 opacity-50">
          <path

    return null

  return (

  )
}

export const CaseStudiesCarousel = () => {
  const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(0)
  const [direction, setDirection] = useState(0)
  const [isAutoPlaying, setIsAutoPlaying] = useState(true)
  const autoPlayRef = useRef<NodeJS.Timeout | null>(null)
  const currentStudy = caseStudies[currentIndex]
  const startAutoPlay = () => {
    if (autoPlayRef.current) clearInterval(autoPlayRef.current)
    autoPlayRef.current = setInterval(() => {
      nextSlide()
    }, 5000)
  }
  const stopAutoPlay = () => {
    if (autoPlayRef.current) {
      clearInterval(autoPlayRef.current)
      autoPlayRef.current = null
    }
  }
  useEffect(() => {
    if (isAutoPlaying) {
      startAutoPlay()
    } else {
      stopAutoPlay()
    }
    return () => stopAutoPlay()
  }, [isAutoPlaying, currentIndex])
  const nextSlide = () => {
    setDirection(1)
    setCurrentIndex((prev) => (prev + 1) % caseStudies.length)
  }
  const prevSlide = () => {
    setDirection(-1)
    setCurrentIndex((prev) => (prev - 1 + caseStudies.length) % caseStudies.length)
  }
  const goToSlide = (index: number) => {
    setDirection(index > currentIndex ? 1 : -1)
    setCurrentIndex(index)
  }
  const slideVariants = {
    enter: (direction: number) => ({
      x: direction > 0 ? 1000 : -1000,
      opacity: 0,
    }),
    center: {
      x: 0,
      opacity: 1,
    },
    exit: (direction: number) => ({
      x: direction < 0 ? 1000 : -1000,
      opacity: 0,
    }),
  }
  return (
    <div
      className="w-full min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-background via-background to-muted/20 flex items-center justify-center py-24 px-8"
      onMouseEnter={() => setIsAutoPlaying(false)}
      onMouseLeave={() => setIsAutoPlaying(true)}
    >
      <div className="max-w-7xl w-full">
        {/* Section Header */}
        <div className="text-center mb-16">
          <h1
            className="text-[40px] leading-tight font-normal text-foreground mb-6 tracking-tight"
            style={{
              fontWeight: "400",
              fontFamily: "var(--font-figtree), Figtree",
              fontSize: "40px",
            }}
          >
            Customer Success Stories
          </h1>
          <p
            className="text-lg leading-7 text-muted-foreground max-w-2xl mx-auto"
            style={{
              fontFamily: "var(--font-figtree), Figtree",
            }}
          >
            See how leading teams use Auralink to gain clarity on collaboration and team alignment.
          </p>
        </div>

        <div className="grid lg:grid-cols-2 gap-12 items-center">
          {/* Left Content */}
          <div className="space-y-8">
            <AnimatePresence mode="wait" custom={direction}>
              <motion.div
                key={currentStudy.id}
                custom={direction}
                variants={slideVariants}
                initial="enter"
                animate="center"
                exit="exit"
                transition={{
                  x: {
                    type: "spring",
                    stiffness: 300,
                    damping: 30,
                  },
                  opacity: {
                    duration: 0.2,
                  },
                }}
                className="space-y-6"
              >
                <div className="text-foreground/60">{currentStudy.logo}</div>

                <h2
                  className="text-4xl font-bold text-foreground leading-tight tracking-tight"
                  style={{
                    fontFamily: "var(--font-figtree), Figtree",
                    fontWeight: "400",
                    fontSize: "32px",
                  }}
                >
                  {currentStudy.title}
                </h2>

                <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
                  {currentStudy.features.map((feature, idx) => (
                    <FeatureBadge key={idx} name={feature} />
                  ))}
                </div>

                <blockquote className="border-l-4 border-primary pl-6 py-2">
                  <p
                    className="text-lg leading-7 text-foreground/80 italic mb-3"
                    style={{
                      fontFamily: "var(--font-figtree), Figtree",
                    }}
                  >
                    "{currentStudy.quote}"
                  </p>
                  <footer
                    className="text-sm text-muted-foreground"
                    style={{
                      fontFamily: "var(--font-inter), Inter",
                    }}
                  >
                    {currentStudy.attribution}
                  </footer>
                </blockquote>
              </motion.div>
            </AnimatePresence>

            {/* Navigation */}
            <div className="flex items-center gap-6">
              <div className="flex gap-2">
                {caseStudies.map((_, idx) => (
                  <button
                    key={idx}
                    onClick={() => goToSlide(idx)}
                    className={`h-2 rounded-full transition-all duration-300 ${idx === currentIndex ? "w-8 bg-primary" : "w-2 bg-muted-foreground/30 hover:bg-muted-foreground/50"}`}
                    aria-label={`Go to slide ${idx + 1}`}
                  />
                ))}
              </div>

              <div className="flex gap-2">
                <button
                  onClick={prevSlide}
                  className="p-2 rounded-lg border border-border hover:bg-accent transition-colors"
                  aria-label="Previous slide"
                >
                  <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
                    <path
                      d="M12.5 15L7.5 10L12.5 5"
                      stroke="currentColor"
                      strokeWidth="2"
                      strokeLinecap="round"
                      strokeLinejoin="round"
                    />
                  </svg>
                </button>
                <button
                  onClick={nextSlide}
                  className="p-2 rounded-lg border border-border hover:bg-accent transition-colors"
                  aria-label="Next slide"
                >
                  <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
                    <path

prompt:
<ROLE=EXECUTOR>
<BUCKET=B>
<UI_INTENT>
{
  "strategy_id": "B1-hero-balanced",
  "primary_focus": "homepage_hero_and_supporting_sections",
  "cta_policy": {
    "timing": "early_and_repeated",
    "intensity": "medium"
  },
  "proof_policy": "concrete_repo_based",
  "content_density": "medium",
  "motion_policy": "subtle_suggested",
  "creative_license": "restricted",
  "language_style": "precise_confident",
  "interaction_restraint": "moderate"
}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Encourage developers building kitchen, food, or restaurant sites to clone and use the Chef's Kitchen Free Next.js landing page template, while clearly explaining what the template includes and how to get started.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating capabilities beyond what is visible in the codebase and README.",
    "Implying this is a hosted product or service rather than a MIT-licensed template repository.",
    "Making performance, SEO, or revenue guarantees that are not supported by the repo.",
    "Suggesting integrations or features (e.g., online ordering, reservations, payments) that are not implemented in the code.",
    "Implying official endorsement or partnership from Next.js, Tailwind CSS, or Vercel."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with clarity that this is a free, MIT-licensed Next.js landing page template for kitchen-related projects.",
    "Keep language developer-focused, highlighting stack, structure, and customization paths rather than marketing hype.",
    "Use only benefits that can be inferred directly from the README, package.json, and provided components.",
    "Balance persuasive CTAs with straightforward instructions on cloning, installing dependencies, and running the dev server.",
    "Keep any motion references subtle and optional, aligning with the existing use of AOS and framer-motion without overselling animations."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not claim built-in e‑commerce, booking, or advanced CMS features that are not present in the repo.",
    "Do not promise business outcomes such as more customers, higher revenue, or improved SEO.",
    "Do not describe this as an official Next.js, Tailwind, or Vercel template.",
    "Do not change the pricing model; keep it clear that this is a free template under the MIT license.",
    "Avoid vague superlatives that cannot be grounded in the codebase (e.g., 'the most powerful restaurant platform ever built')."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1-hero-balanced",
      "intent": "Position the homepage as a clear entry point for developers to understand the template, see its tech stack, and start using it with minimal friction.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers and designers building chef portfolios, food blogs, or restaurant landing pages who want a modern Next.js starter.",
      "information_order": [
        "State clearly that this is a free Next.js landing page template tailored for kitchen and restaurant-style projects.",
        "Highlight key implementation details: built with Next.js app router, React, Tailwind CSS, organized components, and ready-made sections like hero, features, gallery, contact form, auth, and documentation.",
        "Emphasize developer-friendly aspects: clean folder structure, configuration via Tailwind and theme tokens, and quick setup using npm scripts.",
        "Support with concrete proof from the repo such as named components (Hero, Features, Gallery, Documentation), use of next-auth, and the tech stack in package.json.",
        "Close with confident but measured CTAs to clone the repo, install dependencies, and run the development server, optionally mentioning the live demo link."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Cross-check each claimed section (hero, features, gallery, contact, auth, docs) against actual components in src/components and routes in src/app.",
        "Limit stack references to dependencies present in package.json and packages shown in the documentation components.",
        "When mentioning customization, tie statements to Tailwind, typography, color, and logo configuration components rather than generic promises.",
        "Avoid performance or SEO promises; instead describe the template as modern and React/Next.js-based.",
        "Describe motion as subtle and optional, acknowledging existing use of AOS and framer-motion without suggesting complex animations are prebuilt."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2-docs-orientation",
      "intent": "Use the documentation page to reassure developers that the template is easy to navigate, configure, and extend.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers who value clear documentation and want to verify structure and setup before adopting a template.",
      "information_order": [
        "Explain that the template includes a built-in documentation page under the /documentation route.",
        "Summarize what the docs cover: package versions, package structure, quick start steps, and configuration for colors, typography, and logo.",
        "Reinforce that the docs mirror the actual project structure in src/app and src/components, making it easier to follow.",
        "Encourage users to visit the documentation route after cloning to orient themselves and plan customizations."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Align all documentation claims with the existing Documentation, Introduction, PackageStructure, QuickStart, and Configuration components.",
        "Do not imply that the docs cover topics that are not present (e.g., deployment pipelines, CI/CD, SEO playbooks).",
        "Keep tone factual and helpful rather than salesy.",
        "Ensure route names and component names match the code exactly."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B3-auth-lightweight",
      "intent": "Present the sign-in and sign-up pages as optional, ready-made auth screens that can be wired to a real auth backend by the developer.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers who want pre-designed auth pages to plug into their own authentication logic.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify that the template ships with styled sign-in and sign-up pages under the /signin and /signup routes.",
        "Mention that these pages use shared elements like the Breadcrumb component for consistent layout.",
        "Note that next-auth is included as a dependency, giving developers a path to implement authentication if they choose.",
        "Frame these screens as starting points that developers can connect to their own auth flow and providers."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Do not claim that production-ready authentication logic or security hardening is fully implemented.",
        "Refrain from promising compliance or security guarantees.",
        "Keep language to 'includes auth pages and next-auth as a dependency' rather than 'complete auth system'.",
        "Ensure route paths and component names reflect the actual files in src/app/(site)/(auth)."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

{
  "strategy_id": "balanced-landing-copy",
  "primary_focus": "refine the landing page copy for a generic Apex enterprise SaaS starter template with balanced conversion and grounded claims",
  "cta_policy": {
    "timing": "hero_primary_and_secondary_with_footer_reinforcement",
    "intensity": "moderate_confident"
  },
  "proof_policy": "light_social_proof_and_reassurance_without_unverifiable_metrics_or_real_endorsements",
  "content_density": "medium",
  "motion_policy": "reuse_existing_subtle_motion_without_adding_new_complex_animations",
  "creative_license": "restricted_to_wording_and_non_functional_copy_edits",
  "language_style": "precise_confident_neutral",
  "interaction_restraint": "moderate"
}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the copy on the Apex Next.js landing page so it works as a credible, high-quality SaaS starter template with balanced conversion-focused messaging that remains grounded in the repository context.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating real-world adoption with phrases like 'Join thousands of teams' when there is no evidence in the repo.",
    "Implying real endorsements or customer relationships with named brands in the logo marquee.",
    "Introducing medical, legal, or financial promises or guarantees.",
    "Changing the functional structure, layout, or animation behavior of components instead of limiting changes to text.",
    "Diluting the clear value proposition by making the template sound like an unrelated product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Keep all claims clearly compatible with what is visible in the codebase: this is a Next.js SaaS-style landing page template.",
    "Maintain strong but not aggressive CTAs that encourage cloning or using the template, not promising specific business outcomes.",
    "Preserve the premium, enterprise feel of the design with concise, confident language.",
    "Avoid naming specific companies as real customers; if referenced, make their role clearly illustrative or generic.",
    "Do not modify component structure, styling classes, or animation logic; only adjust strings and static copy."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No claims about specific numbers of users, customers, or teams.",
    "No statements that suggest Stripe, Vercel, Linear, Notion, or other brands are actual customers of this project.",
    "No performance guarantees such as revenue growth, uptime guarantees, or legal/compliance assurances.",
    "No changes to TypeScript/JSX logic, props, or Tailwind class structures beyond text content.",
    "No informal, slangy, or overly casual tone that would conflict with an enterprise SaaS aesthetic."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced-landing-copy",
      "intent": "Clarify the Apex value proposition as a polished SaaS starter template, while keeping conversion-friendly hero and CTA copy precise and grounded.",
      "hero_focus": "value_proposition_clarity",
      "information_order": [
        "brand_and_tagline",
        "concise_value_proposition",
        "key_benefit_phrase",
        "primary_and_secondary_ctas",
        "brief_reassurance_or_pricing_note"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or soften any language that implies existing large user bases or live production SLAs.",
        "Frame offers in terms of trying, cloning, or starting from the template, not guaranteed business outcomes.",
        "Keep pricing and plan descriptions generic and obviously template-like without promising real service levels.",
        "Do not introduce any new third-party brand names beyond those already present in the code.",
        "Keep all edits limited to text inside JSX elements and metadata fields."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "template-grounding-and-social-proof",
      "intent": "Reposition social proof and supporting copy so it reads as illustrative for a template rather than literal endorsements, while retaining credibility.",
      "hero_focus": "credibility_without_unverifiable_claims",
      "information_order": [
        "context_as_saas_template",
        "example_use_cases",
        "soft_social_proof_or_familiar_tooling",
        "supporting_cta_and_footer_reassurance"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid phrases that imply real-world adoption scale or specific customer logos as actual clients.",
        "Use wording like 'designed for teams that ship fast' instead of 'used by thousands of teams'.",
        "Do not modify animation behavior of the logo marquee, only adjust its heading and descriptive text.",
        "Ensure footer status and system language sounds illustrative, not as a live service health statement.",
        "Keep all messaging compatible with being a starter kit or template open to adaptation."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

"use client"


interface AnimatedNoiseProps {
  opacity?: number
  className?: string
}

export function AnimatedNoise({ opacity = 0.05, className }: AnimatedNoiseProps) {
  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    const canvas = canvasRef.current
    if (!canvas) return

    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")
    if (!ctx) return

    let animationId: number
    let frame = 0

    const resize = () => {
      canvas.width = canvas.offsetWidth / 2
      canvas.height = canvas.offsetHeight / 2
    }

    const generateNoise = () => {
      const imageData = ctx.createImageData(canvas.width, canvas.height)
      const data = imageData.data

      for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i += 4) {
        const value = Math.random() * 255
        data[i] = value // R
        data[i + 1] = value // G
        data[i + 2] = value // B
        data[i + 3] = 255 // A
      }

      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0)
    }

    const animate = () => {
      frame++
      // Update noise every 2 frames for performance while still looking animated
      if (frame % 2 === 0) {
        generateNoise()
      }
      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(animate)
    }

    resize()
    window.addEventListener("resize", resize)
    animate()

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("resize", resize)
      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId)
    }
  }, [])

  return (
    <canvas
      ref={canvasRef}
      class={className}
      style={{
        position: "absolute",
        inset: 0,
        width: "100%",
        height: "100%",
        pointerEvents: "none",
        opacity,
        mixBlendMode: "overlay",
      }}
    />
  )
</BROKEN_FILE>
